hook.text: Andy Rachleff actually coined the term 'product-market fit' before Andreessen made it famous.

buildup.text: Rachleff was a Stanford professor and co-founder of Benchmark Capital, one of Silicon Valley's top venture firms.

discovery.text: His corollary: the market always wins. If a great team meets a bad market, the market wins. Always.

twist.text: This means picking the right market is more important than having a brilliant team or a clever product.

climax.text: Rachleff's insight shaped a generation of founders: find a desperate market first, then build what it needs.

punchline.text: Great teams lose in bad markets. The market always wins.

quiz.question: What is the Rachleff Corollary?
options: The market always wins — even great teams fail in bad markets
Great teams can overcome any market challenge
Product quality is more important than market size
